headlines.title: Democratic battles and incumbents on defense: What to watch in Tuesday’s primaries
headlines.description: The fight to shape the future of the Democratic Party is playing out in a slew of key primaries Tuesday, as races in six states set the matchups in crucial November battlegrounds and settle personal and ideological battles across the country

headlines.title: Housing official who targeted Trump’s enemies is named director of intelligence
headlines.description: President Donald Trump on Tuesday named an ally with no background in intelligence to oversee the nation’s spy agencies, taking the helm as the U.S. remains at war with Iran after a fresh round of peace talks stalled.

headlines.title: Todd Blanche says DOJ ‘not moving forward’ with ‘anti-weaponization’ fund
headlines.description: Acting Attorney General Todd Blanche said Tuesday that the Justice Department is not moving forward with its $1.8 billion “anti-weaponization” fund.

headlines.title: Israel and Hezbollah trade new attacks despite Trump promise of de-escalation
headlines.description: The Israeli military launched deadly new strikes in Lebanon on Tuesday after accusing Hezbollah of launching attacks overnight, despite President Donald Trump saying both sides had agreed to de-escalate after Iran threatened to pull out of peace talks

headlines.title: Trump to speak at rescheduled White House Correspondents' Dinner
headlines.description: President Donald Trump announced that he would attend the rescheduled White House Correspondets' Dinner in July after the event in April was abruptly cut short when a gunman ran through a security checkpoint. NBC News' Monica Alba reports.

headlines.title: Acting AG Blanche says DOJ is not moving forward with 'anti-weaponization' fund
headlines.description: While testifying at a House Appropriations Subcommittee hearing, Acting Attorney General Todd Blanche said the Justice Department will not be moving forward with the $1.8 billion 'anti-weaponization' fund after a court ruling temporarily blocked it.

headlines.title: Dr. Oz says Trump goes for frequent checkups because he 'likes the results'
headlines.description: Dr. Mehmet Oz, the Head of Centers for Medicare & Medicaid Services, says that President Trump gets frequent health checkups because he "likes the results." Trump’s visit to Walter Reed National Military Medical Center a week ago was the third in-person medical checkup since he took office in 2025.

headlines.title: Massive leadership vacuum weakens U.S. diplomacy in the face of global crises
headlines.description: Hundreds of diplomats have been forced to retire or were laid off in the Trump administration

headlines.title: Extended Interview: Tom Llamas sits down with Secretary of State Marco Rubio
headlines.description: On the sidelines of the Trump-Xi Summit in Beijing, NBC News' Tom Llamas sits down with Secretary of State Marco Rubio to discuss China policy, the war with Iran, the future of Cuba, and more.

headlines.title: Sen. Dan Sullivan accuses Democrats of recruiting Alaska Senate candidate with the same name
headlines.description: Republican Sen. Dan Sullivan’s re-election campaign is accusing one of his opponents in Alaska and Democrats in Washington of recruiting a candidate with the same first and last name: Dan Sullivan.“Mary Peltola and D.C.

headlines.title: U.S. plan to open Ebola quarantine center in Kenya faces growing backlash and protests
headlines.description: Plans for Americans exposed to Ebola to be quarantined abroad faced mounting backlash, with Kenya’s president defending a proposed 50-bed facility in the country after violent protests.

headlines.title: Playing the waiting game in California: From the Politics Desk
headlines.description: Steve Kornacki explains why we may need to practice patience with the results in California, which is hosting major contests for governor, House and mayor.
